design.arm_b: 40 clean items generated (clean mode) + judged live

rate_table.structure: rule -> pair -> battery; rate is catch rate for unknown_flaw/known_flaw, false-positive rate for clean

P3.threshold: catch gain pp > FP rise pp

adoption.adoption_condition: adoption requires BOTH a confirmed P3 AND the recusal design requirement (untestable on this fixture; registered as binding for the live adoption experiment)

tranche1_ledger_context.note: arm A re-used these committed judgments at zero new cost

caveats: Arm A is a zero-token re-aggregation of the tranche-1 raw seat verdicts; per the prereg this file was committed before the first look at per-seat union behavior.

Clean items are generated-as-sound by the tranche-1 adversary model (deepseek-v4-pro) under a soundness prompt and mechanical validation only; soundness was not independently verified, so any genuinely defective 'clean' item inflates the measured false-positive rate (conservative against either-suffices).

Topic-pool mismatch: all 40 clean items draw from the restricted safe-topic pool, while part of the tranche-1 unknown-flaw corpus predates that restriction; catch and FP rates therefore come from overlapping but not identical topic distributions.
P2/P3 verdicts are evaluated on the cross-family pair (the adoption candidate named by P1/P3); the same-family rates are reported in the same table.
Clean-arm seat parse failures: 0 of 160 seat calls (parse failures count as flawed=false, i.e. against catching and against false-positives).
1-1 pair splits are reported per battery; under require-both a split is a no-catch (and a no-flag on clean items), under either-suffices it is a catch (and a false positive on clean items).
Vote-rule adoption is NOT effected by this report: the prereg conditions adoption on P3 AND the recusal design requirement, which this fixture cannot test.
